name: xilinx
description: Xilinx is the leading provider of programmable logic devices and adaptive computing solutions. As an authorized distributor, we offer comprehensive product selection guidance, technical support, and application solutions for Xilinx FPGAs, SoCs, Versal ACAPs, and Alveo accelerator cards.
longDescription: Xilinx, now part of AMD, pioneered the field of programmable logic and continues to lead innovation in adaptive computing. Our product portfolio spans from cost-effective Artix FPGAs to high-performance Versal ACAPs with AI engines. As an authorized Xilinx distributor, we provide end-to-end support including product selection, design consultation, and technical assistance. Our team of experienced FAEs can help you navigate the complete Xilinx ecosystem, from initial architecture selection through production deployment. We stock popular Xilinx devices and provide supply chain continuity for industrial and automotive customers. Whether you need FPGAs for communications infrastructure, Zynq SoCs for embedded vision, Versal ACAPs for AI acceleration, or Alveo cards for data center workloads, we have the expertise and inventory to support your project.
seoTitle: Xilinx Authorized Distributor | FPGA SoC ACAP Selection Guide
seoDescription: Authorized Xilinx distributor offering FPGAs, Zynq SoCs, Versal ACAPs, and Alveo accelerator cards. Technical support and selection guide available.

faqs.question: What Xilinx products are available through your distribution channel?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Xilinx distributor, we offer the complete product portfolio including: 1) FPGAs - Artix-7, Kintex-7, Virtex-7, UltraScale, and UltraScale+ families for programmable logic applications, 2) Zynq SoCs - Zynq-7000 and UltraScale+ MPSoC with integrated ARM processors for embedded systems, 3) Versal ACAP - AI Core and Premium series with AI Engines for next-generation acceleration, 4) Alveo Accelerator Cards - U50, U55C, and U280 for data center deployment. We maintain inventory of popular devices and can support high-volume production requirements with supply continuity programs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for product selection guidance based on your application requirements, performance needs, and budget constraints.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Xilinx FPGA for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Xilinx FPGA involves evaluating several key factors: 1) Logic capacity - estimate required LUTs, flip-flops, and DSP slices for your design, 2) Memory requirements - determine Block RAM needs for buffers and FIFOs, 3) I/O count and standards - count pins and voltage requirements, 4) Transceiver speed - select based on high-speed interface requirements (PCIe, Ethernet, etc.), 5) Power budget - consider thermal constraints and power consumption, 6) Cost targets - balance performance with budget. Artix-7 offers lowest cost, Kintex-7 provides best price-performance, Virtex-7 delivers maximum performance. UltraScale and UltraScale+ offer advanced features for high-bandwidth applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with Kintex-7 for most applications. Use Artix-7 for cost-sensitive designs and Virtex/UltraScale for high-performance requirements. Our FAEs can help with architecture reviews.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Zynq-7000 and Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC?
faqs.answer: Zynq-7000 and UltraScale+ MPSoC differ significantly in architecture and capabilities: 1) Process technology - Zynq-7000 uses 28nm while UltraScale+ uses 16nm FinFET, 2) Processing power - Zynq-7000 has dual-core Cortex-A9 at 667MHz, UltraScale+ has quad-core Cortex-A53 at 1.5GHz plus dual-core Cortex-R5, 3) Graphics - UltraScale+ includes Mali-400 MP2 GPU, 4) Video - UltraScale+ EV variants include dedicated H.264/H.265 codec, 5) Security - UltraScale+ adds advanced security features and TrustZone, 6) Performance - UltraScale+ offers 5x performance improvement with better power efficiency. Zynq-7000 remains cost-effective for basic embedded applications, while UltraScale+ targets AI, vision, and high-performance embedded systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Zynq-7000 for cost-sensitive embedded applications with basic processing needs. Use UltraScale+ MPSoC for AI, computer vision, and applications requiring high-performance heterogeneous computing.

faqs.question: What are Versal ACAPs and when should I use them?
faqs.answer: Versal ACAP (Adaptive Compute Acceleration Platform) represents a new category of heterogeneous compute devices that combine multiple engine types: 1) Scalar Engines - dual-core ARM Cortex-A72 application processors for general-purpose computing, 2) Adaptable Engines - next-generation FPGA fabric with 2x performance improvement, 3) AI Engines - vector processors optimized for machine learning inference delivering up to 8 TOPS per engine, 4) DSP Engines - high-performance signal processing, 5) Network on Chip - high-bandwidth connectivity between all engines. Versal ACAPs deliver 10-100x performance gains for AI inference, 5G baseband processing, and data center acceleration compared to traditional FPGAs. Use Versal when you need maximum compute density for AI, signal processing, or networking applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Versal ACAP for AI inference, 5G infrastructure, radar processing, and data center acceleration. Contact our FAE team for architecture evaluation and migration planning.

faqs.question: How can Alveo accelerator cards improve my data center performance?
faqs.answer: Alveo data center accelerator cards deliver significant performance improvements across multiple workloads: 1) Database analytics - 10-50x faster query processing through FPGA-accelerated filtering and aggregation, 2) AI inference - sub-millisecond latency for real-time applications with batch-1 optimization, 3) Video transcoding - up to 20x faster than CPU-based encoding with broadcast quality, 4) Financial computing - microsecond-scale latency for algorithmic trading and risk analysis, 5) Genomics - accelerated DNA sequence alignment and variant calling. Alveo cards plug into standard PCIe slots and work with Xilinx Runtime (XRT) and Vitis libraries. Cloud providers including AWS, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud offer Alveo-based instances for flexible deployment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Alveo for database analytics, AI inference, video processing, and financial computing in data centers. Start with cloud instances for evaluation, then deploy on-premises for production workloads.

faqs.question: What development tools are required for Xilinx products?
faqs.answer: Xilinx provides comprehensive development tools through the Vivado and Vitis platforms: 1) Vivado Design Suite - complete hardware development environment including synthesis, implementation, simulation, and debug for FPGAs and SoCs, 2) Vitis Unified Software Platform - software development for ARM processors, AI Engine programming, and acceleration kernel development, 3) Vitis HLS - high-level synthesis from C/C++ to RTL for rapid algorithm implementation, 4) Vitis AI - optimized libraries and tools for deploying AI models on Xilinx devices, 5) PetaLinux - embedded Linux development for Zynq and Versal devices. Vivado and Vitis offer free WebPACK editions supporting most devices. Full licenses are only required for high-end Virtex UltraScale+ and Versal Premium devices. All tools run on Windows and Linux platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download free Vivado WebPACK and Vitis for development. Use PetaLinux for embedded Linux projects. Contact our FAE team for tool licensing questions and design methodology guidance.

faqs.question: Do you provide technical support and design services for Xilinx products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Xilinx distributor, we provide comprehensive technical support services: 1) Product selection assistance - our FAEs help choose the right device for your application requirements, 2) Design reviews - schematic and PCB layout review for optimal signal integrity and power delivery, 3) Debug support - assistance with bring-up and troubleshooting using Xilinx tools, 4) Reference designs - access to proven reference designs and application notes, 5) Supply chain management - buffer stock programs and long-term supply agreements for production continuity, 6) Training - workshops and hands-on training for Xilinx tools and design methodologies. Our technical team has extensive experience with Xilinx products across communications, industrial, automotive, and data center applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design cycle for architecture guidance. We can provide reference designs and review your implementation to ensure success.
